blocks.text: The LIN for battery sensors uses a master-slave architecture: only one control unit becomes the master control unit (PGM-FI ECU) and manages all signals on the LIN bus. The slave unit (battery sensor) functions via signals from the master node. If the signal sent using LIN cannot be received for a predetermined period of time or if the information sent using LIN remains abnormal for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When one of the following conditions continues for more than 5 seconds

items.text: The PGM-FI ECU cannot receive the signal from the battery sensor sent using LIN.
When the PGM-FI ECU receives an abnormal signal from the battery sensor sent using LIN.
Disconnection of cord (LIN line) between PGM-FI ECU and battery sensor
Ground fault in code (LIN line) between PGM-FI ECU and battery sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
